String.description: The String class in Apex represents a sequence of characters and provides a comprehensive set of methods for text manipulation including searching, replacing, splitting, formatting, and comparison. Strings in Apex are immutable and use UTF-16 encoding.

contains.description: Returns true if the string contains the specified sequence of characters.

substring.description: Returns a new string that is a substring starting from startIndex up to but not including endIndex.

split.description: Splits the string around matches of the given regular expression, returning up to limit substrings.

replace.description: Replaces each occurrence of the target string in this string with the replacement string.

toLowerCase.description: Converts all characters in the string to lowercase using the rules of the default locale.

trim.description: Returns a copy of the string with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

valueOf.description: Returns the string representation of the specified object, calling its toString method.

List.description: An ordered collection of elements that can contain duplicates. Lists in Apex are zero-indexed and dynamically sized, similar to arrays in other languages. They are the most commonly used collection type for working with sObject records and general-purpose data storage.

add.description: Adds the specified element to the end of the list, increasing its size by one.

get.description: Returns the element at the specified index position in the list.

set.description: Sets the element at the specified index to the given value, replacing the existing element.

remove.description: Removes the element at the specified index and returns it, shifting subsequent elements to the left.

size.description: Returns the number of elements currently in the list.

contains.description: Returns true if the list contains the specified element, using equals for comparison.

sort.description: Sorts the elements in the list in ascending order using the natural ordering of the elements.

Map.description: A collection of key-value pairs where each unique key maps to a single value. Maps in Apex provide efficient O(1) average-case lookup by key and are commonly used for grouping sObject records by Id, deduplication, and building lookup tables from query results.

put.description: Associates the specified value with the specified key, returning the previous value if the key already existed.

get.description: Returns the value to which the specified key is mapped, or null if the key is not present.

containsKey.description: Returns true if the map contains a mapping for the specified key.

remove.description: Removes the mapping for the specified key and returns the previously associated value.

keySet.description: Returns a set view of all keys contained in this map.

values.description: Returns a list of all values contained in this map, which may contain duplicates.

Set.description: An unordered collection of unique elements that does not allow duplicates. Sets in Apex use hash-based storage for efficient membership testing and are commonly used for collecting unique Ids from sObject lists and performing bulk operations.

add.description: Adds the specified element to the set if it is not already present, returns true if the set changed.

contains.description: Returns true if the set contains the specified element.

remove.description: Removes the specified element from the set and returns true if it was present.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the set.

addAll.description: Adds all elements from the specified set or list to this set, returning true if the set changed.

Database.description: The Database class provides static methods for performing DML operations (insert, update, delete, upsert, undelete) with optional partial success handling, executing dynamic SOQL and SOSL queries, and managing savepoints for transaction rollback in Salesforce Apex.

query.description: Executes a dynamic SOQL query string and returns a list of sObject records matching the criteria.

insert.signature: static Database.SaveResult[] insert(sObject[] records, Boolean allOrNone)
insert.description: Inserts records into the database. When allOrNone is false, partial success is allowed and individual SaveResults indicate which records failed.

update.signature: static Database.SaveResult[] update(sObject[] records, Boolean allOrNone)
update.description: Updates existing records in the database with optional partial success handling.

upsert.signature: static Database.UpsertResult[] upsert(sObject[] records, Schema.SObjectField externalIdField, Boolean allOrNone)
upsert.description: Inserts or updates records based on a matching external Id field, combining insert and update into a single operation.

delete.signature: static Database.DeleteResult[] delete(sObject[] records, Boolean allOrNone)
delete.description: Deletes records from the database with optional partial success handling.

setSavepoint.description: Creates a savepoint that can later be used to rollback DML operations to this point in the transaction.

System.description: The System class provides utility methods for debugging, assertions, type information, JSON serialization, and governor limit checks. It is one of the most commonly used classes in Apex for logging, testing assertions, and runtime introspection.

debug.description: Writes the specified message to the debug log at the default log level for developer inspection.

assert.signature: static void assert(Boolean condition, Object msg)
assert.description: Asserts that the specified condition is true; if false, a fatal error is logged and code execution halts.

assertEquals.signature: static void assertEquals(Object expected, Object actual, Object msg)
assertEquals.description: Asserts that the expected and actual values are equal; if not, a fatal error is logged with the optional message.

now.description: Returns the current date and time in the GMT time zone as a Datetime value.

today.description: Returns the current date in the current user's time zone.

Test.description: The Test class provides methods for creating test context, setting up test data, mocking HTTP callouts, and controlling governor limit resets during Apex unit testing. All production Apex code requires at least 75% test coverage for deployment.

startTest.description: Marks the start of the test execution context, resetting governor limits so the code between startTest and stopTest gets a fresh set of limits.

stopTest.description: Marks the end of the test execution context and causes all asynchronous operations started during the test to execute synchronously.

isRunningTest.description: Returns true if the currently executing code was invoked from a test method, useful for conditional test-only logic.

setMock.signature: static void setMock(Type interfaceType, Object mock)
setMock.description: Sets a mock implementation for HTTP callouts or other mockable interfaces during test execution.

Trigger.description: The Trigger context class provides runtime information about the currently executing trigger, including the records being processed, the DML operation type, and whether the trigger is executing before or after the database operation. Trigger context variables are only available within trigger execution.

new.description: Returns the list of new versions of sObject records being processed in insert, update, and undelete triggers.

old.description: Returns the list of old versions of sObject records as they were before the triggering DML operation.

newMap.description: Returns a map of new sObject records keyed by their Id, available in before/after update and after insert triggers.

isBefore.description: Returns true if the trigger is currently executing in the before context, before records are committed to the database.

isAfter.description: Returns true if the trigger is currently executing in the after context, after records have been committed.
